content.problemStatement:
Q6) A .Explain the process of reading from and writing to CSV files in Python using the csv module.
Mention important functions and give an example for each?    ---4 Marks.
constraints: If a CSV file exists it is non‑empty and fits in memory
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a filename (a string without whitespace) of a CSV file located in the same directory as the program.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line. Print "Success" if the program was able to read the entire CSV file and write it back without error. If any exception occurs (e.g., file not found, permission denied) print the exact error message produced by the language runtime.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The file example.csv exists. The program reads all rows and writes them back unchanged, therefore it prints "Success".
The file does not exist, so opening it for reading raises a FileNotFoundError. The program catches the exception and prints its message.
The same existing file is processed ten times. Each iteration succeeds, producing ten lines of "Success".

hidden.label: mixed case
hidden.expectedOutput: Success\n[Errno 2] No such file or directory: 'missing.csv'\n[Errno 13] Permission denied: 'protected.csv'

solution.approach: For each filename we try to open the file for reading. If the open fails we catch the error and output its message. When the file is opened successfully we read all rows (or the whole content) into memory, close the input, then open the same file for writing (truncating it) and write the stored data back unchanged. Finally we output "Success". All operations are wrapped in try/catch (or error‑checking) blocks so that any runtime error is reported exactly as produced by the language.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case where N is the size of the CSV file (number of characters/rows). Total complexity O(T·N).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) to store the file content while rewriting.
